meta.title: TB-CDA-3: Modeling, Visual Analysis, and Governed Publishing

questions.stem: You need the fastest way to reduce rows returned from a database before Tableau renders the view. What should you use?

options.explanation: A data source filter can be pushed to the underlying database early, reducing transferred rows. **A**, **C**, and **D** do not reduce source rows before retrieval. [Optimize Workbook Performance - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/performance_tips.htm)

questions.stem: When should a Tableau extract usually be preferred over a live connection?

options.text: When the database must always answer every interaction in real time
options.explanation: Extracts often improve repeated analysis performance and support offline scenarios. **B** favors live connections, while **C** and **D** are incorrect. [Extract Your Data - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/extracting_data.htm)

options.text: When you want row-level security disabled
When you need more dimensions than Tableau supports
When you need offline use or faster repeated analysis on stable data

questions.stem: For this requirement, what is the key modeling difference between Tableau relationships and physical joins?

options.text: Relationships keep tables separate until query time at the logical layer
options.explanation: Relationships operate at the logical layer and preserve separate tables until analysis time. **B**, **C**, and **D** are incorrect. [The Tableau Data Model - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datasource_datamodel.htm)

options.text: Relationships always duplicate every row immediately
Joins are only available for extracts

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO tasks are common reasons to use a physical join instead of only logical relationships? (Select TWO)

options.text: You need all fields in one denormalized row-level table
options.explanation: Some workflows require explicit row-level flattening. **B** is correct because a physical join creates a combined row structure. **C** describes relationships, and **D** is false because join cardinality still matters. [The Tableau Data Model - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datasource_datamodel.htm)

options.text: You want Tableau to decide table combination at query time automatically
You want to avoid any effect from join cardinality
You must flatten rows before analysis

questions.stem: A text field stores values like `$1,245.75`. What is the safest Tableau approach for calculations?

options.text: Convert it with a calculated field that strips symbols then casts to a number
options.explanation: You typically remove symbols and separators before casting the string to a numeric type. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not produce reliable numeric calculations. [Data Types - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datafields_typesandroles_datatypes.htm)

options.text: Sort it alphabetically and sum the strings
Place it on Color and let Tableau infer currency
Use a dashboard action to change the data type

questions.stem: In this scenario, which Tableau feature groups related dimension members into a single higher-level member without changing source data?

options.explanation: Groups combine members into named categories. **A** creates numeric buckets, **C** controls animation/iteration, and **D** is too specific and not the same concept. [Create Sets - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/sortgroup_sets_create.htm)

questions.stem: For this requirement, what is the primary use of bins in Tableau?

options.text: To create fixed-size buckets for continuous numeric values
options.explanation: Bins segment continuous numeric values into ranges for histograms and similar views. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Create Bins from a Continuous Measure - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_bins.htm)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO statements about data types in Tableau are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: String values with currency symbols may need cleaning before numeric conversion
options.explanation: Date typing affects Tableau date behavior. **B** is correct because dirty currency strings often require cleanup. **C** is false, and **D** confuses geographic roles with data types. [Data Types - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datafields_typesandroles_datatypes.htm)

options.text: Data types only matter after publishing to Server
A geographic role is identical to a data type
Changing a field to date enables date-aware functions and hierarchies

questions.stem: An analyst needs all orders preserved, with returns attached when present and NULL otherwise. Which join is most appropriate if Orders is the left table?

options.explanation: A left join preserves all rows from the left table and matches returns where available. **A** would drop non-returned orders, **C** preserves the opposite side, and **D** stacks rows instead of matching them. [The Tableau Data Model - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datasource_datamodel.htm)

questions.stem: Why might a `FIXED` level-of-detail expression ignore a regular dimension filter?

options.text: Because FIXED LODs only work on extracts
options.explanation: Tableau order of operations evaluates FIXED LOD expressions before regular dimension filters unless those filters become context filters. **B**, **C**, and **D** are incorrect. [Level of Detail Expressions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_calculatedfields_lod.htm)

options.text: Because dimension filters cannot affect any calculations
Because Tableau disables filters on dashboards
Because FIXED LODs are computed before dimension filters unless the filter is in context

questions.stem: In this scenario, which expression type computes an aggregate at a specified level independent of the view’s current level of detail?

options.explanation: LOD expressions explicitly define calculation granularity. **A** is computed after the query result set, and **C** and **D** are not calculation types. [Level of Detail Expressions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_calculatedfields_lod.htm)

questions.stem: For this requirement, what is the main purpose of a parameter in Tableau?

options.text: To provide a user-controlled value that calculations, filters, or reference lines can use

options.explanation: Parameters expose a selectable value that other workbook logic can reference. **A**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO statements about table calculations are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: They are computed after the query result is returned to Tableau
options.explanation: Table calculations operate on the result set in Tableau. **B** is correct because compute-using settings determine direction and restart behavior. **C** and **D** are incorrect. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

options.text: Their addressing and partitioning settings affect the result
They always run before context filters

questions.stem: A running total unexpectedly restarts for each Region pane. What setting should you check first?

options.explanation: Table calculation restart behavior depends on addressing and partitioning. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not control running-total restart boundaries. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which Tableau function category is most appropriate for converting a string date into an actual date value for analysis?

options.explanation: Type conversion and date functions handle parsing and casting to dates. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not the relevant category. [Data Types - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datafields_typesandroles_datatypes.htm)

questions.stem: Why are context filters sometimes used with TOP N filters?

options.explanation: Context filters change the order of operations and can define the data subset before later filters such as Top N. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Tableau's Order of Operations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/order_of_operations.htm)

options.text: To force Tableau to evaluate a prerequisite subset before later filters

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO scenarios are good candidates for LOD expressions? (Select TWO)

options.text: Computing customer lifetime value independent of the current view grain
options.explanation: LODs set explicit granularity independent of the view. **C** is correct because LODs are useful for stable denominators. **B** is more naturally a table calculation, and **D** is layout only. [Level of Detail Expressions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_calculatedfields_lod.htm)

options.text: Finding percent difference from previous mark within a pane
Calculating a fixed denominator such as total sales per region

questions.stem: A calculation using `WINDOW_SUM` returns unexpected values after a sort and pane change. What is the most likely reason?

options.explanation: Window calculations depend on the visual partition and addressing in the current view. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

options.text: The data source must be republished
Tableau requires extracts for all window functions
The table calculation is sensitive to the view structure and compute-using settings

questions.stem: For this requirement, what is the purpose of Measure Names and Measure Values in Tableau?

options.text: To combine multiple measures in one view
options.explanation: Measure Names and Measure Values let multiple measures appear together in one worksheet. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: When building a dual-axis chart, what should you often do if the two axes represent the same unit?

options.explanation: Synchronized axes make comparisons meaningful when units are the same. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not solve the scaling issue. [Dashboards - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/dashboards.htm)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which Tableau capability lets a user click a mark in one sheet and filter another sheet on the dashboard?

options.explanation: Filter actions pass selection context from one view to another. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not perform cross-sheet interaction. [Actions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/actions.htm)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO conditions are important when creating a useful map in Tableau? (Select TWO)

options.text: Latitude and longitude can be used for custom spatial positioning
options.explanation: Geographic roles help Tableau interpret location fields. **B** is correct because coordinates can drive custom maps. **C** and **D** are false. [Maps and Geographic Data Analysis in Tableau - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/maps.htm)

options.text: Dashboards cannot include maps with actions
Fields should have appropriate geographic roles

questions.reference: [Maps and Geographic Data Analysis in Tableau - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/maps.htm)

questions.stem: An analyst wants the percent of total to recalculate after users filter to a subset of products. Which approach is usually best?

options.text: Use a table calculation percent of total over the filtered marks
options.explanation: Table-calculation percent-of-total can respond to the current filtered mark set. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not support interactive recalculation. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

options.text: Convert the measure to a string

questions.stem: In this scenario, which chart type is commonly used to show a distribution of a continuous measure across buckets?

options.explanation: Histograms show distributions across bins. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not the standard distribution view here. [Create Bins from a Continuous Measure - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_bins.htm)

questions.stem: Why might a highlight table be more appropriate than a plain text table?

options.text: It adds color encoding to help users spot magnitude patterns quickly
options.explanation: Highlight tables pair tabular structure with color to reveal patterns. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Dashboards - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/dashboards.htm)

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO dashboard design choices usually improve usability? (Select TWO)

options.text: Keep important KPIs and filters visible without cluttering the canvas
options.explanation: Thoughtful interaction supports exploration. **B** is correct because a clear layout improves comprehension. **C** and **D** often hurt usability. [Dashboards - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/dashboards.htm)

options.text: Hide every legend and title by default
Mix unrelated metrics on one sheet to save space
Use actions and filters deliberately around a clear analytic flow

questions.stem: A scatter plot has millions of marks and is slow to render. Which change is most likely to help first?

options.text: Reduce mark density or aggregate the view before adding more detail
options.explanation: Reducing mark volume or increasing aggregation often gives the biggest first performance improvement. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not directly reduce rendering workload. [Optimize Workbook Performance - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/performance_tips.htm)

options.text: Duplicate the worksheet on the dashboard
Convert all dimensions to discrete dates

questions.stem: For this requirement, what must a user generally have in order to overwrite an existing published workbook on Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud?

options.text: A local administrator account on their laptop
options.explanation: Publishing and overwriting depend on Tableau permissions for the project/content. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not sufficient. [Publish a Workbook - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/publish_workbooks_howto.htm)

options.text: Ownership of the source database server
Appropriate publish/write permissions on the project or content

questions.stem: Why do embedded credentials matter for scheduled refreshes of published data sources?

options.text: They let the server authenticate to the underlying data source when the refresh runs
options.explanation: Scheduled refreshes need valid stored credentials to reach the source system. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Publish a Workbook - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/publish_workbooks_howto.htm)

options.text: They remove the need for permissions

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO statements about Tableau permissions are broadly correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Project-level governance is important for consistent access management
options.explanation: Permissions determine allowed capabilities. **B** is correct because project governance helps apply access patterns consistently. **C** and **D** are false. [Permissions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/server/en-us/permissions.htm)

options.text: Permissions are irrelevant once a workbook is downloaded
A dashboard action can replace content permissions
Permissions control capabilities such as viewing, web editing, and publishing

questions.stem: A published extract refresh fails after the original publisher leaves the company. What is the most likely fix?

options.text: Update the saved credentials or ownership settings on the published data source
options.explanation: Refresh failures after personnel changes usually relate to saved credentials or ownership/permission issues. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not address refresh authentication. [Publish a Workbook - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/publish_workbooks_howto.htm)

questions.stem: For this requirement, what is the main advantage of publishing a certified, shared data source for many analysts?

options.explanation: Shared governed data sources improve consistency and reuse. **B**, **C**, and **D** are incorrect. [Publish a Workbook - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/publish_workbooks_howto.htm)

options.text: It removes the need for permissions
It guarantees every workbook is fast
It promotes trusted reuse of common definitions and connections

questions.stem: In this scenario, which connection style is best when a dashboard must always query the freshest source data directly?

options.explanation: Live connections query the source system directly for current data. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated.

questions.stem: A parameter changes a threshold used in a calculated field, but nothing in the view changes until the calculation is used on the sheet. Why?

options.text: Parameters are disabled for numeric values
options.explanation: Parameters do nothing on their own; they must be referenced by some workbook logic. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

options.text: Parameters only affect the workbook through fields, filters, or analytics that reference them

questions.stem: In this scenario, which feature is most appropriate for guiding users through a sequence of narrative views in Tableau?

options.explanation: Story points present a sequenced narrative of views. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Dashboards - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/dashboards.htm)

questions.stem: A relationship-based model shows duplicated totals after the analyst switches to a physical join at the wrong grain. What is the root issue?

options.explanation: Physical joins can multiply rows when grains differ, which affects aggregated measures. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [The Tableau Data Model - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datasource_datamodel.htm)

options.text: The join changed row cardinality and can multiply measures

questions.stem: In this scenario, which TWO statements about Tableau order of operations are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Context filters are evaluated before regular dimension filters
options.explanation: Context filters run earlier than regular dimension filters. **B** is correct because many filter/LOD interactions depend on operation order. **C** and **D** are false. [Tableau's Order of Operations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/order_of_operations.htm)

options.text: The order of operations can explain surprising interactions between LODs and filters
Color legends are always evaluated before extracts
Order of operations only matters on Tableau Server

questions.stem: A dashboard should let users click a state map and update a detailed sales table plus a trend chart. Which design is the most direct?

options.explanation: Filter actions are designed for cross-sheet interactivity from selected marks. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not implement the requested behavior. [Actions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/actions.htm)

options.text: Convert the table to a story point
Use dashboard filter actions from the map to the target sheets

questions.stem: In this scenario, which area is most relevant when deciding whether a user may publish content to a Tableau project?

options.explanation: Project permissions govern publishing capabilities. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Permissions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/server/en-us/permissions.htm)

questions.stem: Why should dashboard designers usually avoid adding every possible filter and chart to one canvas?

options.text: Because Tableau supports only four filters per dashboard
options.explanation: Clutter makes dashboards harder to understand and can also hurt performance. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false. [Dashboards - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/dashboards.htm)

options.text: Because stories and dashboards cannot coexist
Because visual clutter can hide the main analytic path and hurt performance
